headline: Transcript: Markets surge on Argentine election results
summary: Sonja Hutson speaks to Ciara Nugent and Tim Bradshaw
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of the FT News Briefing podcast episode: ‘Markets surge on Argentine election results’

Sonja HutsonGood morning from the Financial Times. Today is Tuesday, October 28th, and this is your FT News Briefing. American M&A is on a tear and investors seem to be loving Argentina’s election results. Plus, Apple’s services division is expected to hit record revenues.

Tim BradshawIt’s extraordinary that this is a division of Apple’s, which, standalone, is bigger than the entire annual sales of Walt Disney or Tesla or Bank of America or Nestlé.

Sonja HutsonI’m Sonja Hutson, and here’s the news you need to start your day.

[MUSIC PLAYING]

Seems like it was merger Monday yesterday. US companies announced $80bn worth of deals. They happened in some of the country’s biggest industries like banking, biotech, and utilities. The Trump administration has signalled that it’s open to large mergers and acquisitions, and that’s helping revive consolidation.
